rules.title: Checkbox input must have an accessible label
rules.description: Every <input type='checkbox'> must have a non-empty accessible name computable by the accessibility tree. Satisfied by: a <label> wrapping element, an associated <label for='id'>, aria-label on the input, or aria-labelledby pointing to a visible element.
rules.expected: element has accessible name via associated label OR aria-label OR aria-labelledby

conditions: element has id matching a <label for='id'>
element has aria-labelledby whose target has non-empty text content

rules.message: Checkbox input has no accessible label. Wrap in a <label> element or add aria-label to the input.
rules.fix: Wrap the input and visual span inside <label class='checkbox-wrapper'>. Or add aria-label='Option description' directly on the input element.

rules.title: Indeterminate checkbox must declare aria-checked='mixed'
rules.description: When the visual .checkbox has class .checkbox--indeterminate, the associated native input must carry aria-checked='mixed'. The indeterminate property set via JS alone is insufficient; aria-checked='mixed' must be explicitly set.

check.target_input: preceding-sibling input[type='checkbox'].checkbox__input OR parent label input[type='checkbox']

rules.message: Indeterminate checkbox is missing aria-checked='mixed' on the native input. Screen readers will not announce the mixed state.
rules.fix: Add aria-checked="mixed" to the <input> element. Also call inputElement.indeterminate = true in JavaScript.

rules.title: Visual checkbox must be paired with a native input
rules.description: The .checkbox visual span must be preceded by or sibling to an <input type='checkbox' class='checkbox__input'>. A custom visual without a native input is not keyboard operable and breaks screen reader semantics.
rules.expected: a sibling or parent-child <input type='checkbox' class='checkbox__input'> exists in the DOM
rules.message: Visual .checkbox has no associated native <input type='checkbox'>. Keyboard and screen reader users cannot interact with it.
rules.fix: Add <input type='checkbox' class='checkbox__input sr-only' /> as a sibling before the .checkbox span.

rules.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
rules.description: The checkbox stylesheet activates .checkbox__focus-ring via input:focus-visible. Any override that sets display:none or visibility:hidden on the focus ring, or sets outline:none on the native input without a visual replacement, violates WCAG 2.4.7.
rules.expected: element is not hidden when parent input receives :focus-visible; box-shadow is non-null
rules.message: Focus ring is suppressed. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox is focused.
rules.fix: Remove any override that hides .checkbox__focus-ring. Do not set display:none or box-shadow:none on the focus ring element.

rules.title: Disabled checkbox must use correct mechanism
rules.description: The disabled checkbox visual (.checkbox--disabled) must be paired with either: (a) HTML disabled attribute — removes from tab order, or (b) aria-disabled='true' — keeps in tab order for discoverability. Using aria-disabled with tabindex='-1' is contradictory.
rules.expected: associated input has 'disabled' attribute OR has 'aria-disabled=true' without 'tabindex=-1'

allowed_patterns.note: Removes from tab order — standard disabled
Stays in tab order — use when checkbox must be discoverable

invalid_combinations.reason: Contradictory — aria-disabled without HTML disabled is intended to keep element in tab order

rules.message: Disabled checkbox uses aria-disabled='true' with tabindex='-1'. This prevents keyboard discovery of an intentionally in-tab-order element.
rules.fix: Remove tabindex='-1'. Use HTML disabled if you want to remove from tab order entirely.

rules.title: Visual checkbox must not be the sole interactive element
rules.description: A <span class='checkbox'> is not inherently interactive. It must always be paired with a native <input type='checkbox'>. Keyboard and AT users require the native semantics.
rules.message: Visual .checkbox is a <span> with no native input partner. The component is not operable by keyboard.
rules.fix: Add <input type='checkbox' class='checkbox__input sr-only'> before the visual .checkbox span.

rules.title: Colors and sizes must reference design tokens
rules.description: All color, background, border, and opacity values applied to .checkbox elements must use CSS custom properties (var(--token-name)). Hardcoded hex, rgb(), hsl(), or numeric opacity values bypass the token system.
rules.expected: background, color, border-color, box-shadow, opacity all use var(--) — or are transparent, none, inherit, currentColor
rules.message: Hardcoded value '{value}' found on '{property}'. Replace with the corresponding design token.
rules.fix: Replace e.g. background: #1B8354 with var(--checkbox-bg-selected-primary). See token surface in checkbox.contract.md §8.

rules.title: Primitive tokens must not leak into component selectors
rules.description: Component CSS must reference component-level semantic tokens (--checkbox-*), not primitive tokens (--sa-600, --gray-200, --Radius-radius-xs) directly in component selectors.
rules.expected: properties reference --checkbox-* tokens; no --sa-*, --gray-* or other primitive tokens in component selectors
rules.message: Primitive token '{token}' used directly in component selector. Map to a --checkbox-* semantic token first.
rules.fix: Introduce a --checkbox-* token and map it to the primitive in token.css or the component token file.

rules.title: Indeterminate and checked modifiers must not co-exist
rules.description: Applying both .checkbox--checked and .checkbox--indeterminate simultaneously produces undefined visual behavior. When indeterminate, only .checkbox--indeterminate should be present.
rules.expected: no element has both .checkbox--checked and .checkbox--indeterminate simultaneously
rules.message: Both .checkbox--checked and .checkbox--indeterminate are applied to the same element. Remove .checkbox--checked when indeterminate.
rules.fix: Use only .checkbox--indeterminate. The indeterminate state implies checked background by design.

severity_legend.error: Blocks release. Must be resolved before component ships to production.
severity_legend.warning: Must be reviewed. Should be resolved; requires documented justification to waive.
severity_legend.info: Advisory. No action required but noted in audit report.

validation_status_legend.not_run: Rule has been defined but not yet executed against this component.
validation_status_legend.pass: Rule was executed and the component passed.
validation_status_legend.fail: Rule was executed and the component failed. See audit report for details.
validation_status_legend.skipped: Rule was intentionally skipped with documented justification.
validation_status_legend.manual_required: Rule cannot be validated automatically and requires human review.
